scenes.text:
Everyone wants the result.
Nobody wants the process.

It took Amazon nine years to become profitable. It took Colonel Sanders over a thousand rejections before KFC existed. It took Tyler Perry six years
of homelessness before his first hit. What you see as overnight success is years of invisible work, tears, and sacrifice that nobody posted about.

The iceberg illusion is real. People see the tip and think that is
all there is. Underneath is a mountain of failure, persistence, and unglamorous grinding.

Trust the process. Water the seed even
when you cannot see the roots growing.

How long have you been grinding? Share
your journey. Follow for patience fuel.
